ACLRule.description:
A network access control rule on an infrastructure node.

Adapted from CybORG's subnet NACL model. Specifies directional
traffic rules between network segments.
ACLRule.title: ACLRule

Account.description:
A user account on a scenario node.

Distinct from OCR's ``Role`` model: roles map exercise participants
to VM logins for exercise access. Accounts describe the environment
state — what accounts attackers will encounter or exploit.
Account.title: Account

Agent.description:
An autonomous participant in the scenario.

Agents reference existing scenario elements:
- ``entity`` links to the entities section (team/role)
- ``starting_accounts`` links to the accounts section
- ``allowed_subnets`` links to infrastructure entries
- ``initial_knowledge`` references nodes and infrastructure
Agent.title: Agent

AssetValue.description: CIA triad asset valuation for scoring and risk assessment.
AssetValue.title: AssetValue

AssetValueLevel.description: CIA triad value level. Adapted from CybORG ConfidentialityValue.
AssetValueLevel.title: AssetValueLevel

Condition.description:
A monitoring check deployed to a VM.

Either ``command`` + ``interval`` or ``source`` must be set, not both.
Condition.title: Condition

Content.description:
Data or files placed into a scenario node.

Supports three forms:
- ``file``: A single file at a specific path, optionally with inline text.
- ``dataset``: A collection of related items (emails, records, pcaps)
  delivered via a source package or listed as items.
- ``directory``: A directory structure placed at a destination path.
Content.title: Content

ContentItem.description: A single item within a dataset (e.g., one email, one record).
ContentItem.title: ContentItem

ContentType.description: Kind of content placed into a system.
ContentType.title: ContentType

Entity.description:
An organizational unit, team, or person in the exercise.

Entities can nest recursively. Flattened names use dot-notation:
``blue-team.bob`` refers to the ``bob`` entity nested inside
``blue-team``.
Entity.title: Entity

Evaluation.description: A group of metrics with a pass/fail threshold.
Evaluation.title: Evaluation

Event.description: A triggered action combining conditions and injects.
Event.title: Event

Feature.description: A software artifact, service, or configuration deployed to a VM.
Feature.title: Feature

FeatureType.description: Kind of feature deployed to a VM.
FeatureType.title: FeatureType

ImportDecl.description: A module import expanded before full semantic validation.
ImportDecl.title: ImportDecl

InfraNode.description:
Deployment parameters for a node.

Shorthand: ``node-name: 3`` (just the count).
Longhand: full dict with count, links, dependencies, properties, acls.
InfraNode.title: InfraNode

InitialKnowledge.description:
What an agent knows about the scenario at start time.

Adapted from CybORG's INT (Initial Network Topology).
Specifies which hosts, subnets, services, and accounts
the agent has knowledge of before the scenario begins.
InitialKnowledge.title: InitialKnowledge

Inject.description: An action injected between entities during an exercise.
Inject.title: Inject

Metric.description:
A scoring metric — either manual or conditional.

Manual metrics may require artifact submission. Conditional
metrics reference a condition that produces the score.
Metric.title: Metric

MinScore.description:
Pass/fail threshold — either absolute points or percentage.

Shorthand: ``min-score: 50`` (interpreted as percentage).
Longhand: ``min-score: {absolute: 50}`` or ``{percentage: 75}``.
MinScore.title: MinScore

ModuleDescriptor.description: Published module metadata for SDL composition.
ModuleDescriptor.title: ModuleDescriptor

Node.description:
A scenario node — either a VM or a Switch.

The ``type`` field determines which variant is active. VM fields
are only valid when type is VM; Switch nodes carry no extra data.
Node.title: Node

NodeType.description: Whether a node is a virtual machine or network switch.
NodeType.title: NodeType

OSFamily.description: Operating system family. Vocabulary from OCSF Device.os.
OSFamily.title: OSFamily

ObjectiveSuccess.description: Declarative success criteria for an objective.
ObjectiveSuccess.title: ObjectiveSuccess

ObjectiveWindow.description: Optional orchestration window constraining when an objective applies.
ObjectiveWindow.title: ObjectiveWindow

PasswordStrength.description: How resistant the account password is to cracking.
PasswordStrength.title: PasswordStrength

Relationship.description:
A typed directed edge between two named scenario elements.

Source and target can reference any named element in the scenario:
nodes, features, accounts, entities, or infrastructure entries.
The validator checks that both endpoints resolve.

The ``properties`` dict carries type-specific metadata (e.g.,
``trust_type: parent-child`` for AD trusts, ``protocol: SAML``
for federation). It's a flat dict rather than typed sub-models
because relationship properties vary widely and we don't want
to gate expressiveness on pre-modeling every variant.
Relationship.title: Relationship

RelationshipType.description: How two scenario elements relate to each other.
RelationshipType.title: RelationshipType

Resources.description: Compute resources for a VM node.
Resources.title: Resources

Role.description:
A named role on a VM with optional entity assignments.

Shorthand: ``admin: "username"`` (just the username string).
Longhand: ``admin: {username: "admin", entities: ["blue-team.bob"]}``.
Role.title: Role

Script.description:
A timed sequence of events.

Time values are human-readable duration strings parsed to seconds.
Script.title: Script

ServicePort.description: A network service exposed by a node. From OCSF NetworkEndpoint.
ServicePort.title: ServicePort

SimpleProperties.description: Network properties for a switch/subnet: CIDR, gateway, and flags.
SimpleProperties.title: SimpleProperties

Source.description:
Provider-neutral artifact reference.

Shorthand: ``source: "package-name"`` (version defaults to ``"*"``).
Longhand: ``source: {name: "package-name", version: "1.2.3"}``.
Source.title: Source

Story.description: Top-level exercise orchestration — a group of scripts.
Story.title: Story

TLO.description: Training Learning Objective — linked to an evaluation.
TLO.title: TLO

Variable.description:
A named variable for scenario parameterization.

Variables define configurable parameters with types, defaults,
and optional value constraints. They're referenced in other
sections via ``${variable_name}`` syntax.
Variable.title: Variable

Vulnerability.description: A named vulnerability with CWE classification.
Vulnerability.title: Vulnerability

Workflow.description: A declarative experiment control graph over objectives.
Workflow.title: Workflow

WorkflowCompensationFailurePolicy.description: How primary workflow status should treat compensation failures.
WorkflowCompensationFailurePolicy.title: WorkflowCompensationFailurePolicy

WorkflowCompensationTrigger.description: Terminal workflow reasons that may trigger compensation.
WorkflowCompensationTrigger.title: WorkflowCompensationTrigger

WorkflowPredicate.description: Branch predicate over runtime evaluation data and prior step state.
WorkflowPredicate.title: WorkflowPredicate

WorkflowStep.description: A named workflow step with explicit portable control semantics.
WorkflowStep.title: WorkflowStep

WorkflowStepOutcome.description: Portable workflow-visible outcomes emitted by executable steps.
WorkflowStepOutcome.title: WorkflowStepOutcome

WorkflowStepStateRef.description: Predicate reference to previously observed workflow step state.
WorkflowStepStateRef.title: WorkflowStepStateRef

WorkflowStepType.description: Control-flow node types for declarative experiment workflows.
WorkflowStepType.title: WorkflowStepType

WorkflowSwitchCase.description: One ordered branch case within a ``switch`` workflow step.
WorkflowSwitchCase.title: WorkflowSwitchCase

description:
Top-level scenario specification.

A YAML document with up to 21 named sections. Only ``name``
is required. All sections are optional dicts keyed by
user-defined identifiers.
title: Scenario
